title: senecio adnivalis var. erioneuron
intro: Senecio adnivalis var. erioneuron, also known as Mountain Groundsel, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of South Africa, characterized by its woody stems and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids. The health benefits of Mountain Groundsel include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to improve respiratory function and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Mountain Groundsel can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
